intro: Windmannia crenata, also known as Windmannia or Toothed Windmannia, is a plant species belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. The plant is valued for its health benefits, particularly its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Windmannia crenata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Windmannia is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be used to make a tincture, allowing for easy incorporation into a traditional herbal remedy regimen.
